It has a rose gold-plated clip and a rose gold-plated stainless steel #6 JoWo nib and includes a glass eyedropper to fill the pen with your favorite ink. Complemented with an ivory colored finial and end cap, and a translucent light pink/purple cap and barrel, Mocha Mousse is a limited edition design for 2025. An ebonite piston and rubber gasket seals the feed off from the barrel when fully depressed, reducing the risk of leakage and burping. By unscrewing the blind cap and loosening the rod at the rear of the barrel, you open up the ink flow to the feed. matches: Opus 88 Harmony Fountain Pen - Mocha Mousse (Limited Edition) - The Goulet Pen Company
Opus 88 Harmony Fountain Pen - Mocha Mousse (Limited Edition)
Opus 88 Harmony Fountain Pen - Mocha Mousse (Limited Edition)
Opus 88 Harmony Fountain Pen - Mocha Mousse (Limited Edition)

The Opus 88 Harmony fountain pen is a truly unique writing instrument. The Harmony is an eyedropper-filled pen with a piston operated shut-off valve. It has a rose gold-plated clip and a rose gold-plated stainless steel #6 JoWo nib and includes a glass eyedropper to fill the pen with your favorite ink.

An ebonite piston and rubber gasket seals the feed off from the barrel when fully depressed, reducing the risk of leakage and burping. By unscrewing the blind cap and loosening the rod at the rear of the barrel, you open up the ink flow to the feed. During long writing sessions, leave the shut-off valve open to take advantage of the impressive ink capacity. Having a transparent pen barrel allows you to see your current ink level.
The measured length of the visible portion of the nib when it is installed in the pen, from grip to tip.
If a converter is included with the pen, this weight is reflected in the total.
The maximum volume of ink that can fit in the pen when filling the entire barrel of the pen via eyedropper.

matches: Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen - Solid Black - The Goulet Pen Company
Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen - Solid Black
Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen - Solid Black
Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen - Solid Black

This Opus 88 Jazz fountain pen in Solid Black has a black barrel and cap, and is accented by black hardware. This large postable pen is an eyedropper-filled fountain pen with a piston operated shut-off valve. It has a silver stainless steel #6 JoWo nib and includes a glass eyedropper to fill the pen with your favorite ink.

An ebonite piston and rubber gasket seals the feed off from the barrel when fully depressed, reducing the risk of leakage and burping. By unscrewing the blind cap and loosening the rod at the rear of the barrel, you open up the ink flow to the feed. During long writing sessions, leave the shut-off valve open to take advantage of the impressive ink capacity. An ink window allows you to see your remaining ink in the barrel.
Note: JoWo is transitioning their nib designs from the old scroll pattern to a more simple design. Stock is currently mixed; what you receive may not necessarily match what is photographed.
The measured length of the visible portion of the nib when it is installed in the pen, from grip to tip.
If a converter is included with the pen, this weight is reflected in the total.
The maximum volume of ink that can fit in the pen when filling the entire barrel of the pen via eyedropper.
JoWo nib units. The  came well tuned and smooth. I swapped in an  and the pen didn’t miss a beat.

Eyedropper fill… the shutoff valve is a godsend. Not for flying or altitude change or whatever… but for everyday use. Most eyedropper filled pens will start to burp when 1/3 to 1/2 full simply from the warmth of your hand expanding the air bubble. The valve completely eliminates that. Keep the valve closed and write with the ink in the section. If the nib starts to write a little dry, crack the valve open a half turn to allow the section to fill back up and then close up again. And off you go. I’ve had zero issues and I’ve written enough to refill the barrel once. So 4.5ml of ink’s worth of writing.

If you want the capacity that filling your barrel offers, but you are nervous about the drawbacks of eyedropper filling, this is the way. I have multiple pens that are either eyedropper by design or I have converted. This is the best “pure” eyedropper system. This valve or the “pseudo-eyedropper” system of Noodler’s .308 refillable cartridges are great options to get that huge fill and limit burping.
Large Ink Capacity is a Major Bonus!
Opus 88 is my top fav eyedropper fountain pen

Opus 88 is my top fav eyedropper fountain pen. I can write for weeks with one ink fill-up! Smooth writing and easy maintenance. It’s perfect. A classy, all-black EDC fountain pen. No brainer. VERY happy.

This is my first Opus 88, plus my first flex nib, and I couldn't be more pleased with it. The Jazz is well balanced either posted or unposted, feels well-made despite being lightweight, and writes like a dream. Although I have nothing to compare it to (so take this with a grain of salt), the flex nib has a pleasing amount of give without being overly soft. I am thrilled with my purchase.

sources.title: Opus 88 Harmony Fountain Pen – Fountain Pen Revolution
extracts: eyedropper-filled pen with a piston operated shut-off valve. It comes with an eyedropper and is packaged in attractive Opus 88 boxing. An ebonite piston and rubber gasket seals the feed off from the barrel when fully tightened. This keeps the pen from leaking which is a common drawback of eyedropper-filled pens. To open ink flow to the nib simply loosen the rod inside the body by twisting the knob at the end of the barrel counterclockwise. For longer writing sessions or when flex writing it is important to keep the valve open. Note: Since these pens are #6 JoWo threaded they will also accept our FPR-FNF ultra flex nib units featuring ebonite feeds . Features: Eyedropper-filled (eyedropper included) #6 Jo

Opus 88 Harmony Red Grey Marble Mechanism

OPUS 88 have released two pens in their new range, Harmony, which feature a Rose Gold plated nib and trim – a first for Taiwan-based OPUS 88. “Brown” offers several shades and tones which pair very nicely with the rose gold appointments and the “Red Grey Marble” is a very sleek and elegant resin.
The pens have an eyedropper filling system which give the pen a large ink holding capacity and they are straightforward to fill using the pipette provided in the packaging.
The #6 steel Jowo nibs are available in Fine, Medium and Broad.
OPUS 88 Harmony Mocha Mousse Fountain Pen

sources.title: Just a moment...
Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen Review — The Pen Addict
extracts: ink capacity, and plenty of fun colors to choose from. I've come to love Opus 88 for their unique pen shapes and different (but attractive) color schemes, but the Jazz fits a more traditional spot compared with their other models. The Jazz is still a full-size pen — pretty much the same length and diameter as the Demo or Koloro. It features a cigar shape with a tapered body and large cap that suits the #6 nib well. The blue colorway I have has two acrylic materials that complement each other and present a m
matches: Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen Review — The Pen Addict

pens recently. I really enjoy using the Demo and Fantasia models, but I've also picked up several Koloro and Minty models since they have unique designs. As if I needed yet another Opus 88 model to try out, I now find myself with a blue Jazz pen on my desk vying for attention.

is a more traditional style fountain pen that still features some interesting materials and Opus 88's default eyedropper filling mechanism. Like other Opus 88 pens, you get a lot of bang for your buck, lots of ink capacity, and plenty of fun colors to choose from.

I've come to love Opus 88 for their unique pen shapes and different (but attractive) color schemes, but the Jazz fits a more traditional spot compared with their other models. The Jazz is still a full-size pen — pretty much the same length and diameter as the Demo or Koloro. It features a cigar shape with a tapered body and large cap that suits the #6 nib well. The blue colorway I have has two acrylic materials that complement each other and present a more sleek and professional look.

If it's an Opus 88 pen, then it features a humongous ink reservoir that uses an eyedropper to fill. The Jazz can fit close to 2ml of ink in the reservoir, which gives you lots and lots of writing time. When you're not writing, the butt of the pen screws down to seal the ink reservoir from the nib feed, ensuring the ink stays fresh in between writing sessions (and also making it unaffected by atmospheric pressure changes). Opening the ink reservoir enough to write is just a matter of unscrewing the butt of the pen a couple turns to allow the ink to flow to the feed. And, when it's time to clean out the pen, it's easy to flush with water since the reservoir opening is so large.

When it comes down to writing with the pen, it's a really comfortable experience for my hand and grip. The grip section is a nice size and has plenty of surface area to hold onto. Even though it's a smooth surface, the polished acrylic has plenty of grip to easily control the pen. Given the large size, I think this will work well with a large variety of different grips and hand sizes. The cap posts on the back of pen securely, but I find this to add too much weight to the top. The pen is a perfectly balanced weight when the cap isn't posted.

The #6 JoWo nib is fairly plain in terms of decoration, but it performs beautifully. The fine nib on this unit is smooth and precise, and the ink feeds perfectly. It's a steel nib with a fairly small tip, so the writing line is crisp and thin, but there's just enough spring in the nib to make it really comfortable to use — it doesn't feel like writing with a rigid nail at all. It's a great middle-ground of a cushioned feel along with crisp, controlled lines.

The blue colorway I have here is on option, but there are several other versions (both solid and semi-translucent) of the Jazz if blue isn't your thing. In terms of nibs, you have a wide selection from the typical EF to B as well as a couple small flex nibs or a 1.5mm italic. All of the nib options are steel, but you can easily swap these nibs out for something else pretty easily.
As of this writing, the Jazz pens are on sale for

. Even at the normal price of $125, these pens are an excellent value. And, if the cigar-like shape of the Jazz isn't your favorite, there are so many other options from Opus 88 that will deliver the same excellent writing experience and massive ink capacity for a similar price.

Note: Since these pens are #6 JoWo threaded they will also accept our FPR-FNF ultra flex nib units featuring ebonite feeds . Features: Eyedropper-filled (eyedropper included) #6 JoWo nib units Screw on cap Unique ink shut
matches: Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen – Fountain Pen Revolution
Opus 88 Jazz Fountain Pen - Clear / Extra Fine
The Opus 88 Jazz fountain pen is a large eyedropper-filled pen with a piston operated shut-off valve.  It comes with an eyedropper and is packaged in attractive Opus 88 boxing.

An ebonite piston and rubber gasket seals the feed off from the barrel when fully tightened.  This keeps the pen from leaking which is a common drawback of eyedropper-filled pens.  To open ink flow to the nib simply loosen the rod inside the body by twisting the knob at the end of the barrel counterclockwise.  For longer writing sessions or when flex writing it is important to keep the valve open.
Note: Since these pens are #6 JoWo threaded they will also accept our
FPR-FNF ultra flex nib units featuring ebonite feeds
Unique ink shut off valve to prevent leaking.

Made in Taiwan, these new Opus 88 fountain pens feature a huge ink capacity as well as a unique, Japanese style eye-dropper filling mechanism. The pen has an ink shut off value to prevent ink from leaking into the cap during travel or changes in air pressure. During longer
writing sessions, leave the shut off valve open so ink can make its way down into the feed.
